definition: Grimoire builds well-nested element open/close trees with siblings and attributes that advance the xmlTextReader state machine into XML_TEXTREADER_END/BACKTRACK/XML_ELEMENT_NODE states that cmplog byte mutations never assemble.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's gap-abstraction and structural recombination build well-formed XML with sibling element nodes, driving reader->node to an XML_ELEMENT_NODE with a following sibling; cmplog's byte/I2S mutations produce syntactically present XML but never the required sibling-element tree shape.
signature.one_line: Grimoire builds sibling-element XML tree shape needed for XML_ELEMENT_NODE branch

analysis.summary_one_line: Well-formed XML where an element node having a following sibling is itself an empty/childless XML_ELEMENT_NODE during forward traversal

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A valid XML document whose DOM produces, while xmlTextReaderRead traverses forward from an XML_TEXTREADER_ELEMENT state, a node that is an XML_ELEMENT_NODE with a non-NULL next sibling (reader->node->next != NULL at L1326) AND whose type is XML_ELEMENT_NODE at L1328. The branch needs the tree to actually be built into element nodes with sibling structure, not merely a syntactically present XML header.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire wins 10/10 and is the only fuzzer to enter the L1328 true side at all (W shows T=1 F=3 vs L T=0 F=20). The GeneralizationStage abstracts byte runs of a coverage-preserving XML corpus entry into <GAP> tokens and the Grimoire string/structural mutators recombine those generalized fragments, so it can synthesize new well-formed element/sibling tree shapes that drive reader->node to an element node with a following sibling, taking the true branch and reaching L1335-1337 (W-exclusive lines).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog reaches L1327 (node->next != NULL) but never satisfies L1328 (L: T=0 F=20) — its seeds are mutated XML text (valid <?xml version="1.0"?> headers, DOCTYPE) but the resulting parse tree does not present an XML_ELEMENT_NODE with a following sibling at the traversal point; cmplog's byte-level havoc + I2S + token mutators perturb literals/bytes but do not construct the required sibling-element grammar structure, so it falls through to the L1370/L1384 backtrack path (L-exclusive lines).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the single-technique delta over cmplog is grimoire's GeneralizationStage (<GAP> abstraction) plus the Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireExtension/RecursiveReplacement/StringReplacement) that splice generalized token/gap structures. This grammar-free structural recombination builds new well-formed XML element/sibling shapes that cmplog's byte/I2S/token mutators cannot. This is structural-mutation attribution, not comparison-logging substitution: both fuzzers already run the same input-to-state stage, so I2S cannot be the divergence axis.

evidence_trail.claim: The lone decisive pair is grimoire > cmplog with delta grimoire_structural, the single-technique difference being grimoire's added stages over the shared cmplog/I2S stack.
The blocker condition requires the current node to be an XML_ELEMENT_NODE while a following sibling exists.
Grimoire resolves the branch in every trial while cmplog never does.
Only the winner takes the true side of the L1328 blocker; the loser takes it zero times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1328  T=1 F=3  T=0 F=20  (reader->node->type == XML_ELEMENT_NODE) &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-exclusive lines downstream of the blocker set XML_TEXTREADER_END and jump to node_found, the resolving path.
Grimoire's added technique over cmplog is the GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators that splice generalized token/gap structures (grammar-free structural mutation).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: Both grimoire and cmplog run I2S, so I2S cannot be the divergence axis between them.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Loser cmplog seeds contain valid XML text (version header, DOCTYPE) yet still block the branch.
The winner grimoire seed differs structurally from the loser cmplog seeds across the document body, not just at a single literal offset.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Per-offset byte sets. Format: hex(ascii)xCOUNT. Shows offsets where W and L bytes differ AND at least one side is concentrated

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness exposing the same node->type==XML_ELEMENT_NODE-with-following-sibling gate in which cmplog resolves the branch as often as grimoire — showing the divergence comes from byte/I2S reach rather than from grimoire's structural recombination of well-formed XML.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: No grimoire-discovered W lineage chain or generalized <GAP> artifact is shown in the prompt, so the structural-recombination mechanism is inferred from technique knowledge plus the L1328 coverage split rather than directly observed in this seed's history.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's RecursiveReplacementMutator recombines generalized token/gap structures to produce nested element-with-attribute markup (e.g. `<l n=""><b ...>`) that makes the parser create a childless XML_ATTRIBUTE_NODE; cmplog's mutations stay in DOCTYPE territory and never enter the attribute-node loop.
signature.one_line: Grimoire splices element-attribute markup producing childless attribute node

analysis.summary_one_line: Reaching an attribute node with no children requires a well-formed XML element-with-attribute structure that grimoire's structural recombination builds and re-tries.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A syntactically valid XML element carrying an attribute whose value parses to an XML_ATTRIBUTE_NODE with an empty (children==NULL) value, e.g. element-open + attribute-name + quoted value markup such as `<l n=""><b ...>`, so the reader loop reaches xmlTextReaderReadAttributeValue on a childless attribute node.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The grimoire winner seeds carry intact XML element/attribute markup produced by structural splicing — `<l n=""><b ...>` appears at offset 0x10 onward, and lineage shows the leaf was built by GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator recombining generalised token/gap structures rather than mangling raw bytes. The branch true side is taken 25 times (avg hitcount=25) and resolved in 10/10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser seeds are byte/token mutations of a fixed `<?xml version="1.0"?> <!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/...` skeleton (offsets 0x10-0x40 are DOCTYPE boilerplate), so the parser stays in DOCTYPE/external-entity territory and the attribute-node loop never produces a childless XML_ATTRIBUTE_NODE: at the blocker cmplog shows T=0 F=11 and blocked 9/10 trials for 23.30h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so the single-technique delta is grimoire's GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators: generalising corpus bytes into token/gap templates and recursively splicing them lets grimoire synthesise the nested element-with-attribute markup that constructs a childless attribute node, which per-offset byte substitution alone (cmplog) does not assemble.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker true side requires an attribute node whose children pointer is NULL.
The winning grimoire seeds contain valid XML element-with-attribute markup.
The winning seed was produced by a Grimoire structural mutator.
The loser cmplog seeds are byte/token mutations of a fixed DOCTYPE/dtds skeleton.
At the blocker the loser never takes the true side while the winner does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2826  T=9 F=1  T=0 F=11  if (reader->curnode->children == NULL)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire resolves the branch in all trials while cmplog blocks in nine of ten.
The single-technique delta is grimoire's generalization plus structural mutators on top of the shared cmplog/I2S stack.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire mutators splice and recurse on generalised token/gap structures, a grammar-free structural mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only blocker is reaching a childless attribute node, where cmplog resolves it as often as grimoire (no grimoire advantage), would show the divergence is not driven by structural recombination.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that cmplog stays stuck in DOCTYPE/external-entity parsing rather than the attribute loop is inferred from seed byte layout, not from a per-role trace of where cmplog inputs actually diverge in the parser.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's structural mutators generate well-nested element open/close documents that advance the reader into XML_TEXTREADER_END/BACKTRACK states; cmplog's byte/I2S/token mutations fail to synthesize balanced nested element structure so the end state is never reached.
signature.one_line: Grimoire builds balanced nested elements advancing reader to END/BACKTRACK state

analysis.summary_one_line: Well-formed nested-element XML that drives the reader into an END/BACKTRACK state on an element node, taking the end-element true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML document with nested element open/close tags (e.g. ) so the reader, after descending into an element, reaches reader->state == XML_TEXTREADER_END or XML_TEXTREADER_BACKTRACK while node->type is XML_ELEMENT_NODE, hitting line 2952's true side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry intact XML grammar fragments — DOCTYPE/SYSTEM declarations plus element bodies like '><a><b f=""><' — produced by recursive structural splicing of generalized token/gap structures, so the reader walks far enough to enter the end/backtrack state and the branch true count is non-zero (W T=3 at L2952).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds stall in the early document prolog/parse loop; at the blocker line their true count is zero (L T=0 F=23) and they never reach an element-end state, because byte-level I2S/havoc/token mutation does not synthesize the balanced nested open/close element structure required to advance the reader to XML_TEXTREADER_END/BACKTRACK.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus entries into <GAP>/token structures and the Grimoire structural mutators (RecursiveReplacement, RandomDelete) splice grammar-shaped fragments, generating well-nested element documents that cmplog's byte-oriented havoc+token+i2s stack cannot assemble; since the input-to-state stage is present in both fuzzers, the divergence is attributable solely to grimoire's structural mutation layer.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of an end-element state test on an XML_ELEMENT_NODE.
Taking the true side returns the END_ELEMENT reader type, reached only after element close.
The decisive pair is grimoire>cmplog with the grimoire_structural single-technique delta.
Grimoire resolves the branch in all 10 trials while cmplog blocks in all 10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=0/10  blocked=10  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: Winner grimoire seeds contain nested XML element structure produced by Grimoire structural mutators.
Winner seed lineage is driven by Grimoire recursive replacement and delete mutators.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2952  T=3 F=10  T=0 F=23  if ((reader->state == XML_TEXTREADER_END) ||  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser cmplog seeds are byte/token mutations of the document prolog, not nested element structure.
Both grimoire and cmplog include the I2SRandReplace stage, so I2S is not the differentiator.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
grimoire builds on the full cmplog stack —
it includes the `CmpLogObserver`, the `TracingStage`, and the
`I2SRandReplace` (i2s) stage — and ADDS a `GeneralizationStage` plus
Grimoire structural mutators

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire's added layer is structural generalization plus grammar-free structural mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on reaching an element-end/backtrack state shows cmplog resolving it as fast as grimoire, i.e. no divergence when the target requires balanced nested open/close tags rather than a single literal byte gate.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that cmplog's stall is specifically the inability to synthesize balanced nested open/close element structure rests on seed-byte inspection, not on a counted structural metric of element nesting depth.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's RecursiveReplacement and RandomDelete mutators splice element fragments into deeper well-formed trees (nested <a><b> with empty attribute), driving the reader into a terminal childless element traversal that exposes the true branch; cmplog's byte blobs never form nested element structure.
signature.one_line: Grimoire splices nested element fragments for terminal childless XML_ELEMENT_NODE traversal

analysis.summary_one_line: Well-formed nested XML with an empty/childless element reached via the sibling-exhausted traversal path so oldstate==ELEMENT and node->type==ELEMENT_NODE both hold

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML document with nested element nodes that, during streaming reader traversal, lands on an XML_ELEMENT_NODE having no children and no NODE_IS_EMPTY flag, while the previous state was XML_TEXTREADER_ELEMENT and node->next is NULL (sibling list exhausted). This requires the parser to build a real element tree deep enough to back-track over a terminal childless element.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire winner seeds carry coherent element grammar: seed 1 holds `<!DOCTYPEa SYSTE`/`td"><a><b f=""><` (nested <a><b> elements with an empty attribute), so the reader walks into element children and exhausts siblings, driving the true branch at L1371 — its hitcount on the branch is 4 vs the loser's 0, and DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows W reaches L1371 T=2 while L is T=0.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds are byte-mangled length-prefixed blobs (`06 00 00 00 31 32 37 37 37 32 .xml`) that never assemble the nested element structure; at the blocker line L records T=0 F=14, meaning the reader repeatedly evaluates the predicate but node->type is never XML_ELEMENT_NODE on that traversal path, so the true side stays unvisited across all 10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator, GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator) splice and recurse on generalized token/gap structures, so grimoire can recombine whole element fragments into deeper well-formed trees. cmplog has the same I2S stage but only byte-level havoc/token mutation, so it cannot synthesize the nested element nesting; the divergence is the grimoire-structural delta, not I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the node->type == XML_ELEMENT_NODE conjunct of a guard whose enclosing condition also requires oldstate==XML_TEXTREADER_ELEMENT, no children, and NODE_IS_EMPTY clear.
The winning grimoire seed contains nested element structure with an empty attribute element.
The winner takes the true branch (hitcount 4) while the loser never does (hitcount 0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=4  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker line the winner reaches the true side while the loser stays entirely on the false side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L1371  T=2 F=0  T=0 F=14  (reader->node->type == XML_ELEMENT_NODE) &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser seeds are length-prefixed byte blobs rather than coherent nested XML.
grimoire's structural mutators recombine generalized token/gap structures, a capability cmplog lacks despite both having I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: The single-technique delta between grimoire and cmplog is generalization plus Grimoire mutators, not I2S, since both run I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If cmplog (havoc + I2S only, no structural recombination) resolves this branch at a comparable rate to grimoire on a synthetic harness requiring nested-element traversal, the grimoire-structural attribution is wrong and the gate is reachable by byte-level mutation alone.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I infer the loser never assembles nested elements from its mangled byte layout, but I did not directly confirm via lineage that grimoire's structural mutators (rather than its co-resident I2S stage) are what produce the winning nested structure.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire builds empty-element-followed-by-sibling XML trees (e.g. nested `<a><b f=""><`) driving reader->state to XML_TEXTREADER_END, taking the false side; cmplog's byte mutations produce degenerate text-expansion paths where state is never END.
signature.one_line: Grimoire constructs empty-element-with-sibling tree driving reader to END state

analysis.summary_one_line: XML with an empty element having a following sibling, so the reader reaches XML_TEXTREADER_END state and takes the false branch

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML document containing an empty/childless element node that is immediately followed by a sibling node (or is itself empty), so that xmlTextReaderRead sets reader->state = XML_TEXTREADER_END (lines 1335 or 1374) before reaching the blocker at 1505; the false side of (reader->state != XML_TEXTREADER_END) is taken only when state == XML_TEXTREADER_END.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire-discovered winner seeds carry valid structural XML (e.g. Seed 1: '<?l ?><!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"><a><b f=""><' with nested elements). grimoire's GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators splice generalised token/gap structures, building nested element shapes that drive the reader into the END-state arms; the W-only lines 1335/1374 (reader->state = XML_TEXTREADER_END) are hit only on winner runs, and the blocker false side shows L1505 F=3 for W vs F=0 for L.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog seeds remain in a degenerate parse (e.g. Seed 1 'wwwwwwwwwwwwml\' run of 'w' bytes prefixing the XML) that exercises the text/CDATA expansion path (L-only lines 1437-1439, xmlTextReaderExpand) instead of the empty-element-with-sibling arms; reader->state never becomes XML_TEXTREADER_END so the blocker's false side is never taken (L1505 T=26 F=0). cmplog's byte-level I2S substitution cannot synthesize the nested element-with-sibling tree structure that grimoire's structural recombination produces.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: grimoire builds on the full cmplog/I2S stack and ADDS a GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator, GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator, etc.) that splice generalised token/gap structures grammar-free. This structural recombination, absent from plain cmplog, assembles the empty-element-followed-by-sibling tree shape that drives the reader to XML_TEXTREADER_END; the single-technique delta over cmplog is precisely generalization + Grimoire mutators.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is grimoire>cmplog with delta grimoire_structural and prob_div 1.00.
The blocker's false branch is taken only when reader->state equals XML_TEXTREADER_END.
The state is set to XML_TEXTREADER_END only on winner-resolving (W) source lines, in the empty-element-with-sibling arm.
A second W-only END-state arm fires for an empty element node with no children.
Winner grimoire seeds carry well-formed nested XML element structure.
Loser cmplog seeds carry a degenerate 'w'-byte run prefixing the XML and never take the blocker false side (L1505 F=0 for L).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L1505  T=10 F=3  T=26 F=0  ((reader->state != XML_TEXTREADER_END) &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds instead exercise the text/CDATA expansion path that is loser-only.
grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators on top of the full cmplog/I2S stack, which is the single-technique delta.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
it includes the `CmpLogObserver`, the `TracingStage`, and the
`I2SRandReplace` (i2s) stage — and ADDS a `GeneralizationStage` plus
Grimoire structural mutators

evidence_trail.claim: The Grimoire mutators splice and recurse on generalised token/gap structures via string-based grammar-free structural mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness gating on reader->state == XML_TEXTREADER_END (an empty element followed by a sibling) were resolved by plain cmplog as fast as by grimoire, with no advantage from structural recombination, the grimoire-structural attribution would be refuted.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that grimoire's structural recombination specifically builds the empty-element-with-sibling tree (rather than just producing more valid XML by chance) is inferred from seed structure and source, not directly traced through lineage.
